courses.title: Psychiatric & Mental Health Nursing
outcomes: Explain neurobiological and psychosocial factors in mental health disorders.
Demonstrate therapeutic communication and nurse-patient relationship building in psychiatric settings.
Formulate nursing care plans for mood, anxiety, psychotic, substance-use, and personality disorders.
Apply psychopharmacology principles, monitoring side effects and patient safety.
Identify psychiatric emergencies, suicide risk assessment, and crisis intervention techniques.

courses.title: Critical Care & Adult Health Nursing II
outcomes: Assess critically ill patients using hemodynamic monitoring, ABG analysis, and mechanical ventilation parameters.
Plan care for acute coronary syndromes, acute respiratory distress, shock, sepsis, and multi-organ dysfunction.
Apply rapid response protocols, cardiac arrest resuscitation (BLS/ACLS principles), and emergency triage.
Demonstrate safe management of IV vasoactive infusions, invasive lines, and critical care drug administration.

courses.title: Epidemiology in Health and Disease
outcomes: Calculate and interpret epidemiological measures of disease frequency (incidence, prevalence, mortality rates).
Explain disease transmission models, outbreak investigation steps, and disease surveillance systems.
Evaluate epidemiological study designs (cross-sectional, case-control, cohort, randomized trials).
Apply epidemiological principles to communicable and non-communicable disease control in Pakistan.

courses.title: Biostatistics and Health Informatics
outcomes: Apply descriptive and inferential statistical methods to health and nursing data.
Interpret p-values, confidence intervals, t-tests, chi-square, and regression analyses in research papers.
Demonstrate health informatics tools, electronic health records (EHR), and nursing data management systems.
Apply data privacy, security, and ethical guidelines in digital health records.
